title: Release docs: add a way to bypass the CI skips
body:
Background: As of #23174, CI is selectively run depending on
the set of files changed. Changes to version.go should require
a full run of all tests, because we want releases tested.

Problem: RC releases bump version.go and then bump it back.
Cirrus's changesInclude() sees this as a NOP, so it does
not trigger the "run all CI tests" rule.

Solution: require CI:ALL in release PRs. Add step to process.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
I kind of dislike this (the notion of adding comment changes as a way to force CI to run), but from a convenience perspective it's the easiest way to make these PRs not a No-op. LGTM
I keep thinking there's got to be a better way, but it eludes me.

Oh good, this is ugly...
So thinking about this the special case only happens on rc's that bump the -dev back to the original value. Maybe we can change that practise? I am not sure how "defined" these suffixes are but I could see something like `5.2.0-dev-rc1` or something like that.

Or we "simply" remember to use CI:ALL for such release PRs?
I really hate the multi-suffix thing. CI:ALL is reasonable in my opinion?
One other idea I have, is the title of the release PR consistent? Then we could match based on the release title without requiring the author to add a special tag.
I have always named the release PR "Bump to" - but our vendor updates also sometimes use that?
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
